mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower corporate borrowing costs and equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistically significant positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 5.5%, labor market slack triggers recession fears that compress equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 60-day returns remain positive in 70% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 5.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in retail sales and housing starts signal a synchronized contraction in consumer demand, which forces earnings downgrades and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Joint declines in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict negative 20-day returns with a rank IC greater than 0.02 over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of rapid industrial production growth, supply-side momentum fuels short-term equity rallies as corporate output capacity expands, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth fails to predict positive 5-day returns during recovery phases, with out-of-sample accuracy below 55%.

mechanism_narrative: Steady increases in total nonfarm payrolls reflect expanding economic activity and rising corporate revenues, which consistently support positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows zero or negative correlation with 20-day forward returns across multiple market cycles.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation above 2.5% annualized forces central banks to tighten policy expectations, raising discount rates and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 60-day returns remain positive in 65% of months where CPIAUCSL exceeds 2.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between quarterly GDP growth and monthly retail sales indicates weakening consumer spending relative to overall output, which triggers earnings revisions and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The GDPC1-RSAFS divergence metric yields a rank IC below 0.01 for 60-day forward returns over any 3-year rolling period.

mechanism_narrative: During early-cycle expansions, housing starts lead broader economic activity, and declines in HOUST signal stalled recovery momentum, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: HOUST declines fail to predict negative 20-day returns during early-cycle phases, with a hit rate below 45% over a 5-year sample.
